channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zim PSL 2026 Matchday 23 fixture info: Bosso host Scottland @3menonaboat
item.description:
Here is the full fixture of Zim PSL matches this weekend

The post Zim PSL 2026 Matchday 23 fixture info: Bosso host Scottland first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: UK Triple Murder Suspect Found With Illegal Firearm in South Africa as Extradition Process Begins
item.description: JOHANNESBURG — A British citizen of Zimbabwean heritage wanted in the United Kingdom over the alleged murders of his wife and two daughters has been found in possession of an illegal firearm following his arrest in Johannesburg, as South African authorities begin formal extradition proceedings. Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, 45, appeared before the Johannesburg Magistrate’s Court [...] The post UK Triple Murder Suspect Found With Illegal Firearm in South Africa as Extradition Process Begins appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Alice Chiota Retains Carling Black Label National Women’s Pool Title, Continues Dominance
item.description: Alice Chiota cemented her status as Zimbabwe’s leading female pool player on Saturday night, successfully defending her Carling Black Label National Women’s Pool Championship after defeating Diana Kabasa in a thrilling final at Urban Lifestyles Arena in Masvingo. The 41-year-old Chitungwiza cueist produced a composed performance to retain her national crown, overcoming a strong [...] The post Alice Chiota Retains Carling Black Label National Women’s Pool Title, Continues Dominance appeared first on ZIMBUZZ. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbuzz

item.title: Film-maker and actor Trueman Chikukwa thoroughly beaten and robbed at gunpoint by InDrive driver and his goons
item.description: Harare – In a chilling incident that has once again cast a spotlight on the safety of ride-hailing services, prominent Zimbabwean actor and filmmaker Trueman Chikukwa was brutally assaulted at gunpoint following a dispute with an InDrive driver. The attack, which left Chikukwa with a swollen face, occurred as he and his wife were returning [...] The post Film-maker and actor Trueman Chikukwa thoroughly beaten and robbed at gunpoint by InDrive driver and his goons first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Professor Herbert Chinyanga Dies
item.description: By A Correspondent | Zimbabwe has lost one of its pioneering medical professionals following the death of Professor Herbert Mapfumo Chinyanga,... Continue reading "Professor Herbert Chinyanga Dies" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe Government to Review Taxes and Levies to Ease Business Costs,
item.description: Zimbabwe Government to Review Taxes and Levies to Ease Business Costs Zimbabwe is set for a fresh review of taxes and levies as Government moves to reduce pressure on citizens and businesses while creating a more investor-friendly economy. Minister of Justice, Legal and Parliamentary Affairs Ziyambi Ziyambi said the review was aimed at preventing citizens [...] The post Zimbabwe Government to Review Taxes and Levies to Ease Business Costs, appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Coach Rambo in Deadly Accident: Suffers Broken Ribs, Punctured Lung and Head Injuries
item.description: The stretch of road between Norton and Harare has long been a site of both transit and tragedy, but for the biker community and the nation at large, it recently became the scene of a terrifying incident involving one of Zimbabwe’s most celebrated modern-day heroes. Victor Kazembe, popularly known as “Coach Rambo,” a man who [...] The post Coach Rambo in Deadly Accident: Suffers Broken Ribs, Punctured Lung and Head Injuries first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: The Invisible Currency: Why Expectations Matter More Than Banknotes
item.description: Money is one of humanity’s greatest inventions, yet its value rests on something we cannot see. A banknote is little more than paper. A balance in a bank account is merely a collection of electronic records. Even gold derives much of its value not from its practical uses, but from what people collectively believe it [...] The post The Invisible Currency: Why Expectations Matter More Than Banknotes appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mnangagwa Says 2030 Presidential Term Extension Wasn’t His Idea
item.description: President Mnangagwa says extending his presidential term to 2030 was not his idea, blaming others as debate intensifies nationwide today. The post Mnangagwa Says 2030 Presidential Term Extension Wasn’t His Idea appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Thousands of Zimbabwean children face disrupted education after returning home from South Africa
item.description: The South African More than 10 000 Zimbabwean children who left South Africa following anti-migrant protests and subsequent displacement are facing an education crisis after their schooling was disrupted midway through the academic year. Many are now struggling to continue their studies after returning to Zimbabwe, where the curriculum differs from South Africa’s CAPS syllabus. The Zimbabwe [...] The post Thousands of Zimbabwean children face disrupted education after returning home from South Africa appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Watchdog pushes for political finance reforms as Africa marks Anti-Corruption Day
item.description: TRANSPARENCY International Zimbabwe (TIZ) has called for stricter political finance laws and faster anti-corruption action as Zimbabwe joined the continent to mark Africa Anti-Corruption Day over the weekend. This year’s theme, “Scaling Up the Promotion of Integrity and Anti-Corruption Actions Across Africa,”calls for moving beyond commitments to concrete results, TIZ said. The organisation warned that [...] The post Watchdog pushes for political finance reforms as Africa marks Anti-Corruption Day appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: You can’t be judge and complainant, High Court tells Chief Murinye in school row @zimsituation
item.description: ‘This is a text-book case where this court must invoke its inherent power to stop a grave injustice’ Source: You can’t be judge and complainant, High Court tells Chief Murinye in school row – Zimbabwe News Now Chief Murinye HARARE – The High Court has ruled that a traditional chief cannot act as both complainant [...] The post You can’t be judge and complainant, High Court tells Chief Murinye in school row appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Express Load truck overturns, family survives by whisker @bulawayo24
item.description: HARARE — A South African‐registered truck belonging to Express Load overturned early Sunday morning after the driver reportedly failed to negotiate a curve while speeding.The truck, which was carrying... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Why do African countries change their names?
item.description: Why did the Gold Coast become Ghana, Rhodesia become Zimbabwe and Swaziland become Eswatini? African countries have usually changed their names when they entered a new political era, using new names to reflect independence, national identity and a break from colonial rule. (via The African Exponent) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The African Exponent

item.title: Jannik Sinner beats Alexander Zverev to win Wimbledon again and move on from French Open meltdown
item.description: LONDON — Jannik Sinner is starting to make a habit of responding to adversity in Paris with titles at Wimbledon. The top-ranked Sinner beat Alexander Zverev 6-7 (7), 7-6 (2), 6-3, 6-4 Sunday for his second consecutive title at the All England Club after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue following a [...] The post Jannik Sinner beats Alexander Zverev to win Wimbledon again and move on from French Open meltdown appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail
